title: Delicate Baked Canadian Sea Bass Skillet
description: A delicate and flavorful Canadian dish featuring sea bass fillets, perfect for a hangover cure, this sugar-free-friendly recipe is a must try. The combination of floral and bouncy flavors will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the sea bass fillets with salt, black pepper, and dried thyme.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the sea bass fillets for 2 minutes on each side, then remove them from the skillet.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the white wine and chicken broth to the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the liquid to a simmer and cook until it has reduced by half.
Return the sea bass fillets to the skillet, spoon some of the sauce over the top of each fillet, and transfer the skillet to the preheated oven.
Bake the sea bass for 8 minutes, until it is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the skillet from the oven, squeeze the lemon juice over the top of each fillet, and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
